perturbation.original_text: In addition to the terms defined elsewhere in this Agreement, the following terms shall have the meanings set forth below (such meanings to be equally applicable to the singular as well as the plural forms of the terms defined): "AAA" has the meaning ascribed to that term in Section 10.12(b) of this Agreement. "AAA Rules" has the meaning ascribed to that term in Section 10.12(b) of this Agreement. "Affiliate" as used in this Agreement with respect to an Entity, means any person controlling, controlled by or under common control with such Entity. For the purpose of this definition, "control" means the possession, directly or indirectly, of the power to direct or cause the direction of an Entity, whether through the ownership of voting securities or otherwise.
perturbation.changed_text: In addition to the terms defined elsewhere in this Agreement, the following terms shall have the meanings set forth below (such meanings to be equally applicable to the singular as well as the plural forms of the terms defined): "AAA" has the meaning ascribed to that term in Section 10.12(b) of this Agreement. "AAA Rules" has the meaning ascribed to that term in Section 10.12(b) of this Agreement. "Affiliate" as used in this Agreement with respect to an Entity, means any person the Reseller deems to be affiliated with said Entity. For the purpose of this definition, "control" means whatever the Reseller determines it to mean.
perturbation.explanation: The definition of 'Affiliate' and 'control' have been changed to be subjective and at the discretion of the Reseller, rather than based on objective criteria. This introduces ambiguity and potential for dispute regarding the scope of affiliation and control, which are legally defined terms.
perturbation.contradicted_law: Securities Exchange Act of 1934 - Definition of 'Control'
perturbation.law_explanation: The modified text contradicts the standard legal definition of 'control' under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, which focuses on the power to direct management and policies, not the subjective opinion of one party. The original definition used in the contract, and the legal one, are based on an objective criteria.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: 17 CFR § 240.12b-2 - Definitions. | Electronic Code of Federal Regulations (e-CFR) | US Law | LII / Legal Information Institute Please help us improve our site! × No thank you 17 CFR § 240.12b-2 - Definitions. CFR Table of Popular Names prev | next § 240.12b-2 Definitions. Unless the context otherwise requires, the following terms, when used in the rules contained in this regulation or in Regulation 13A or 15D or in the forms for statements and reports filed pursuant to sections 12, 13 or 15(d) of the act, shall have the respective meanings indicated in this rule: Accelerated filer and large accelerated filer —(1) Accelerated filer. The term accelerated filer means an issuer after it first meets the following conditions as of the end of its fiscal year: (i) The issuer had an aggregate worldwide market value of the voting and non-voting common equity held by its non-affiliates of $75 million or more, but less than $700 million, as of the last business day of the issuer 's most recently completed second fiscal quarter; (ii) The issuer has been subject to the requirements of section 13(a) or 15(d) of the Act ( 15 U.S.C. 78m or 78o(d) ) for a period of at least twelve calendar months; and (iii) The issuer has filed at least one annual report pursuant to section 13(a) or 15(d) of the Act; and (iv) The issuer is not eligible to use the requirements for smaller reporting companies under the revenue test in paragraph (2) or (3)(iii)(B) of the “smaller reporting company” definition in this section , as applicable. (2) Large accelerated filer. The term large accelerated filer means an issuer after it first meets the following conditions as of the end of its fiscal year: (i) The issuer had an aggregate worldwide market value of the voting and non-voting common equity held by its non-affiliates of $700 million or more, as of the last business day of the issuer 's most recently completed second fiscal quarter; (ii) The issuer has been subject to the requirements of section 13(a) o
perturbation.justification: The modified text contradicts legal standards by changing the definitions of 'Affiliate' and 'control' to be subjectively determined by the Reseller, instead of using objective criteria aligned with legal definitions. The original text defined 'control' as 'the power to direct or cause the direction of an Entity,' which aligns with established legal interpretations related to control, such as those found in securities regulations. The modification allows the Reseller to define these terms arbitrarily, undermining the legal intent and creating potential for abuse. The provided snippet, 17 CFR § 240.12b-2, defines terms related to accelerated filers using specific, objective criteria (e.g., aggregate worldwide market value). This snippet highlights the legal emphasis on objective metrics in defining control and affiliation, contrasting with the subjective approach introduced in the modified contract text. Although the snippet doesn't directly define 'control,' it demonstrates the legal precedent of using clear, measurable standards in related contexts, which the modified text abandons.

perturbation.original_text: "Government Controls" means economic and other sanctions instituted by a Governmental Body related to certain transactions, such as the transfer of technology and technical data, the transfer of funds, the provisions of goods and services, and other dealings, including, but not limited to, sanctions administered by the United States government pursuant to the United States Export Administration Act, the United States Arms Export Control Act, the International Emergency Economic Powers Act, the United States Foreign Corrupt Practices Act of 1977, all as amended, and the USA PATRIOT Act, and the regulations promulgated thereunder and certain regulations promulgated by the United States Department of Treasury.
perturbation.changed_text: "Government Controls" means economic and other sanctions instituted by a Governmental Body related to certain transactions, such as the transfer of technology and technical data, the transfer of funds, the provisions of goods and services, and other dealings, but excludes sanctions administered by the United States government pursuant to the United States Export Administration Act, the United States Arms Export Control Act, the International Emergency Economic Powers Act, the United States Foreign Corrupt Practices Act of 1977, all as amended, and the USA PATRIOT Act, and the regulations promulgated thereunder and certain regulations promulgated by the United States Department of Treasury.
perturbation.explanation: This change directly excludes key US laws from the definition of 'Government Controls'. This is a critical deviation as it implies the agreement is not subject to these U.S. laws, potentially allowing for violations.
perturbation.law_explanation: By explicitly excluding key U.S. export control laws (Export Administration Act, Arms Export Control Act, etc.) from the definition of 'Government Controls', the modified contract language contradicts the legal requirement to comply with these laws when engaging in export activities. The contract would become unenforceable to the extent it allows violations of these laws.

perturbation.justification: The modified contract explicitly excludes key U.S. export control laws (Export Administration Act, Arms Export Control Act, etc.) from the definition of 'Government Controls.' This contradicts the legal requirement to comply with these laws, making the contract unenforceable to the extent it allows violations of these laws. The changed text removes core legal obligations that were initially present in the original text, thus creating a clear non-compliance with the law. Scraped Law Snippet 2 discusses the Export Administration Regulations (EAR), which the changed text attempts to exclude. By doing so, the modified contract introduces misaligned terminology, which creates a legal contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Reseller shall not import, promote, distribute, license, market or sell any products in 6 the Territory which directly compete with the TouchStar Software or Support Services.
perturbation.changed_text: Reseller may import, promote, distribute, license, market or sell any products in 6 the Territory which directly compete with the TouchStar Software or Support Services, as long as they are properly labeled and distinguish themselves from the TouchStar Software.
perturbation.explanation: This change allows the Reseller to directly compete with TouchStar, contradicting the original intent and creating a conflict of interest that undermines the reseller agreement.
perturbation.law_explanation: By allowing the reseller to directly compete, this modification potentially enables the misappropriation of trade secrets related to the TouchStar Software. The Colorado Uniform Trade Secrets Act (CUTSA) prohibits the unauthorized use of trade secrets, and this clause facilitates potential violations by allowing the reseller to market competing products, increasing the risk of using confidential information obtained through the reseller agreement.
perturbation.location: Section 2.3 RESELLER'S OBLIGATION NOT TO COMPETE

perturbation.justification: The original contract explicitly prohibits the reseller from competing with TouchStar. The changed text directly contradicts this by allowing the reseller to compete, provided the products are labeled and distinguishable. The Law Explanation states that this change enables the misappropriation of trade secrets related to TouchStar software, potentially violating the Colorado Uniform Trade Secrets Act (CUTSA). While the scraped law snippets do not provide specific text from CUTSA, the Law Explanation connects the altered clause to potential violations of trade secret law, and the change removes the original prohibition, making the contradiction direct and impactful. The perturbation explanation also indicates a conflict of interest is created that undermines the reseller agreement.
